mechanism_narrative: A persistently low or falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when policy stays accommodative rather than tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than unconditional returns during periods with FEDFUNDS below 1% or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening labor income and lower cyclical demand, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A broad acceleration in retail sales and housing starts indicates stronger domestic demand, improving revenue expectations and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both rise over their 3-month averages
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either proxy alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO signals stronger industrial production and better corporate operating leverage, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next week when manufacturing momentum is already positive.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum is followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the signal's short-horizon hit rate falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Falling CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS remains near zero improves real financial conditions and supports equity multiples, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation rate declines and FEDFUNDS < 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation plus near-zero policy does not outperform either condition alone, or if triggered 20-day SPX returns are not significantly above baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in PAYEMS signals payroll growth and stronger household income, which should support equity demand and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if payroll growth does not correspond to higher-than-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized downturn in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies demand contraction that should weaken earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns negative and RSAFS declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined downturn signal is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases or if the joint signal adds no predictive power over either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: After a sustained rise in FEDFUNDS, equity valuations reprice downward through higher discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week when policy moves into tightening territory.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 0.25pp over 2 consecutive meetings
failure_condition: Reject if post-hike 5-day SPX returns are not below baseline, or if the hit rate for negative returns after the trigger is not above 55%.
